speaker: ANTONY
text: He will not fight with me, Domitius?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: No.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Why should he not?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: He thinks, being twenty times of better fortune,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: He is twenty men to one.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tomorrow, soldier,

speaker: ANTONY
text: By sea and land I’ll fight. Or I will live

speaker: ANTONY
text: Or bathe my dying honor in the blood

speaker: ANTONY
text: Shall make it live again. Woo’t thou fight well?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I’ll strike and cry “Take all.”

speaker: ANTONY
text: Well said. Come on.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Call forth my household servants.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let’s tonight

speaker: ANTONY
text: Be bounteous at our meal.—Give me thy hand;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou hast been rightly honest.—So hast thou,—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou,—and thou,—and thou. You have served me well,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And kings have been your fellows.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What means this?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: ’Tis one of those odd tricks which sorrow shoots

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Out of the mind.

speaker: ANTONY
text: And thou art honest too.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I wish I could be made so many men,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And all of you clapped up together in

speaker: ANTONY
text: An Antony, that I might do you service

speaker: ANTONY
text: So good as you have done.

speaker:
ALL
               
              THE
               
              SERVITORS
text: The gods forbid!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Well, my good fellows, wait on me tonight.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Scant not my cups, and make as much of me

speaker: ANTONY
text: As when mine empire was your fellow too

speaker: ANTONY
text: And suffered my command.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What does he mean?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To make his followers weep.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tend me tonight;

speaker: ANTONY
text: May be it is the period of your duty.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Haply you shall not see me more, or if,

speaker: ANTONY
text: A mangled shadow. Perchance tomorrow

speaker: ANTONY
text: You’ll serve another master. I look on you

speaker: ANTONY
text: As one that takes his leave. Mine honest friends,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I turn you not away, but, like a master

speaker: ANTONY
text: Married to your good service, stay till death.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tend me tonight two hours—I ask no more—

speaker: ANTONY
text: And the gods yield you for ’t!

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: What mean you, sir,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To give them this discomfort? Look, they weep,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And I, an ass, am onion-eyed. For shame,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Transform us not to women.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ho, ho, ho!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Now the witch take me if I meant it thus!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Grace grow where those drops fall! My hearty friends,

speaker: ANTONY
text: You take me in too dolorous a sense,

speaker: ANTONY
text: For I spake to you for your comfort, did desire you

speaker: ANTONY
text: To burn this night with torches. Know, my hearts,

speaker: ANTONY
text: I hope well of tomorrow, and will lead you

speaker: ANTONY
text: Where rather I’ll expect victorious life

speaker: ANTONY
text: Than death and honor. Let’s to supper, come,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And drown consideration.
